best_for: Master roadmap, strategic reasoning, uploaded file context, decision support, commercial judgement, quality control.
use_when: The task needs to reason across NEXT.io, Strait Up Growth, AI, Singapore, Chinese, questionnaire answers, and the uploaded files.
avoid: Loose one-off drafting. Do not ask it for a mega-plan when you need one decision.
fallback: Claude Chat or Claude Project for document-heavy writing.
notes: Projects keep chats, files and custom instructions together for long-running work.

best_for: Source-backed market scans, competitor checks, AI and tech research, tool comparisons, trend reports.
use_when: You need current evidence before making a recommendation or choosing a tool.
avoid: Fast drafting, internal comms, or work where sources are already fixed.
notes: Use when the output must cite sources and be checked against current information.

best_for: Complex online tasks with navigation, connected data sources, uploaded files, forms and spreadsheet edits under user control.
use_when: The task requires actions across websites, files or apps, and you can supervise.
avoid: Sensitive actions without approval, confidential CRM actions, or anything that should be manual first.
fallback: Claude Cowork for local files, Make or n8n for repeatable automations.
notes: Use only after defining the task, approval rules and failure conditions.

best_for: Recurring reminders, weekly briefings, Chinese practice nudges, Friday review prompts, simple scheduled actions.
use_when: The task is recurring and prompt-based, not a complex workflow.
avoid: Complex file work, sensitive automations, or tasks needing app navigation.
fallback: Gemini Scheduled Actions or calendar reminders.
notes: Good for reminders and structured recurring prompts, not real process automation.

best_for: Long-form writing, email refinement, document packaging, tone matching, converting rough thinking into polished material.
use_when: You need words, structure, tone, or document polish.
avoid: Final source-backed factual research without checking sources.
notes: Use for writing and packaging, not for blindly validating strategy.

best_for: Multi-step knowledge work on local files, document prep, file management, folder sorting, research synthesis.
use_when: You want Claude to work with local files or desktop context and complete a defined knowledge-work task.
avoid: High-risk file changes without a backup, confidential actions without permission, broad goals with unclear output.
fallback: ChatGPT Agent or manual folder review.
notes: Anthropic positions Cowork as agentic knowledge work, not normal chat.

best_for: Interactive HTML tools, calculators, dashboards, scorecards, prompt runners, simple apps.
use_when: You need a quick usable internal tool without building a full app.
avoid: Final business strategy or anything requiring a stable backend before the workflow is proven.
fallback: Google AI Studio for Gemini-native prototypes, Lovable for full-stack web apps.
notes: Good for simple internal tools after the task is clear.

best_for: Packaged instructions for repeatable workflows, document conventions, brand voice, operating procedures.
use_when: You want Claude to follow the same workflow reliably each time.
fallback: ChatGPT Project instructions or a repo AI_CONTEXT file.
notes: Build only after the process is proven.

best_for: Broad external research, Google Search-backed scans, Workspace-connected research if enabled, tool discovery.
use_when: You need broad current information or Google ecosystem context.
avoid: Final judgement without a critic or source check.
notes: Use for market and AI-tech scanning.

best_for: Quick Google-connected help, search-oriented brainstorming, lightweight learning, Android or Google ecosystem convenience.
use_when: The task benefits from Google context but does not require deep research.
avoid: Private or sensitive company data without approved controls.
notes: Use for light tasks, not your master system.

best_for: Routine scheduled updates or reminders inside Gemini.
use_when: You already use Gemini and need lightweight scheduled nudges.
avoid: Core operating system or anything that cannot tolerate inactivity or platform changes.
notes: Useful but should not become another notification layer.

best_for: Gemini experiments, Build mode prototypes, multimodal tests, API prompt testing, realtime voice/video experiments, prompt-to-code exploration.
use_when: Gemini-native behaviour matters or you want to test an AI app idea before building elsewhere.
avoid: Stable business dashboards, final roadmap, or app builds when the workflow is not proven.
fallback: Claude Artifacts for simple tools, Lovable for full-stack web apps.
notes: Exploration and AI-native prototyping. It sits before Lovable in the build ladder.

best_for: Learning from fixed source packs, uploaded documents, websites, YouTube, audio, Google Docs and Slides, plus Audio Overviews.
use_when: You have a fixed source pack and want grounded learning or audio summaries.
avoid: Live market scans as your only source or broad strategic decisions without critique.
notes: Best for learning and synthesis from known sources.

category: Fast source-backed web and browser assistant
best_for: Fast source-backed checks, current web answers, browsing support, early source gathering.
use_when: You need a quick web-informed first pass.
avoid: Sensitive browser automation, confidential work, or final decisions without source checking.
fallback: Gemini Deep Research or ChatGPT Deep Research.
notes: Agentic browsers can introduce security risk. Keep sensitive work elsewhere.

best_for: Ruthless critique, alternative reasoning, technical reasoning tests, cheaper second opinions.
use_when: You want another perspective after ChatGPT or Claude, not another mega-plan.
avoid: Primary operating system, confidential work, connected workflow tasks.
notes: Use for critique, not storage of your business context.

best_for: Working web apps, internal tools, portals, dashboards, Supabase-backed products, simple SaaS concepts.
use_when: The workflow is proven and you need frontend, backend, auth, database or deployment.
fallback: Claude Artifacts or Google AI Studio depending on job.
notes: Build only after the manual workflow earns it.

best_for: Visual automations, AI agents, integrations across business apps, repeatable operational workflows.
use_when: A manual workflow has proved value and needs to run across apps.
avoid: Messy processes with unclear owners or bad data.
notes: Great after process clarity. Dangerous before it.

best_for: Flexible workflow automation, AI workflow design, technical automations with more control.
use_when: You want deeper control than simple no-code tools and can manage workflow logic.
avoid: Anything you cannot maintain or secure.
notes: Good for agentic workflow automation after manual proof.
